claims.claim: The TR maximum temperature of the NTM (sodium-ion) battery is 511.7°C and the maximum temperature rise rate is 2285.5°C/min, both intermediate between the LFP and NCM batteries.
claims.quote: The TR maximum temperature and the maximum temperature rise rate of the NTM battery are 511.7 ◦C and 2285.5 ◦C/min, respectively, which are intermediate between the LFP and NCM batteries.

claims.claim: Under natural convection conditions (U = 10 W m⁻² K⁻¹), a fully charged NTM battery can spontaneously ignite if the storage temperature exceeds 169.6°C.
claims.quote: Under natural convection conditions, a fully charged NTM battery can spontaneously ignite if the storage temperature exceeds 169.6 ◦C.

claims.claim: The activation energy (Ea) of the NTM battery is 0.8840 × 10⁵ J/mol, which is lower than both the LFP battery (1.0993 × 10⁵ J/mol) and the NCM battery (1.0461 × 10⁵ J/mol), indicating higher thermal reactivity for the NTM battery.
claims.quote: The values of Ea and A for the NTM battery notably undershoot those recorded for both LIBs. Specifically, the Ea of the LFP battery and NCM battery surpasses that of the NTM battery by 24.4 % and 18.3 %, respectively, suggesting a higher thermal reactivity for the NTM battery.

claims.claim: The TNT equivalent energy released during thermal runaway of the NCM battery (2.635 g TNT) is 2.2 times higher than that of the NTM battery (1.212 g TNT), while the NTM and LFP batteries (1.204 g TNT) are nearly identical.
claims.quote: the W calculated for the NTM battery, LFP battery and NCM battery are 1.212, 1.204, and 2.635 g TNT, respectively. The results indicate that the W of the NTM battery is very similar to that of the LFP battery (the former is only 0.7 % more than the latter), while the W of the NCM battery sample battery is 2.2 times higher than that of the NTM battery.

claims.claim: When the surface heat transfer coefficient U increases from 0 to 200 W m⁻² K⁻¹, the SADT of the NTM battery increases from 124.8°C to 240.1°C, showing the most significant safety improvement among the three battery types.
claims.quote: As U increases, the SADT of the three types of batteries increases, but the rate of increase, d(SADT)/dU, then decreases, and the rate of increase of the SADT of the NTM battery is always greater than that of the remaining two batteries.
